response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with roughly 19 years of development experience and a genuine passion for testing and developer tooling, but this is a Level II people-management role and the resume shows no formal people-management or organizational-leadership experience at all. The minimum requirements of 2+ years people management and 3+ years in a technical leadership role are not evidenced; his trajectory is consistently senior/staff IC and open-source maintainer. The dev-tools and mobile exposure are real but scattered, and do not compensate for the missing management track record.
strengths: Deep, hands-on developer-tooling and testing background (open-source test frameworks, CI/CD setup, Selenium training, MCP code generators)
19+ years of broad engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and high-throughput backend systems
Teaching and community leadership (workshops, courses, code katas) suggesting mentoring aptitude
concerns: No formal people-management experience, versus a hard requirement of 2+ years
No sustained technical-leadership role (3+ years) or experience leading organizational change
Developer-tools work is fragmented across roles and side projects rather than a dedicated multi-year focus on build/test infrastructure at scale
